# Object Store - Full Documentation

# Object Store v2 Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Object Store v2 lets CloudHub applications store data and states across batch processes, Mule
components and applications, from within an application or by using the Object Store REST API.

You can select Object Store v2 as the implementation for Mule 4 in CloudHub by checking the **Object
Store V2** checkbox in Runtime Manager at deployment time.

See the [FAQ](osv2-faq) for Object Store information.

![A diagram illustrating how Mule and user-defined components connect to a central data store](_images/osv2-architecture.png)

On-premises Mule applications can access Object Store v2 only via the Object Store REST API.

You can configure a Mule app to use the Object Store REST API to store and retrieve values from an
object store in another Mule app.

## Object Store v2 Features

- Supports both static and rolling [TTL](#time-to-live) (time to live) for data persistence.
- Supports using the Connected Apps feature to authenticate with Anypoint Platform using APIs.

  See [Configuring Connected Apps](osv2-connected-apps).

- Allows for an unlimited number of entries. There is no limit on the total size of v2
  object-stores.
- Stores values up to 10 MB (when Base64 encoded) in size. You can estimate base64 size of a payload
  as follows: CEILING(base10size \* 1024/3) \* 4, where base10size is object size in bytes. +
  Example: A base10 payload size of 7.5 MB converts to 10.24 MB base64.
- Is available in all supported regions and availability zones within each region.
- Is co-located in the same region as your workers. For example, workers hosted in Singapore would
  use Object Store v2 hosted in Singapore.
- Provides a Mule connector and REST interface for access by external applications.
- Provides end-to-end secure TLS-transport.
- Encrypts persistent storage to FIPS 140-2 compliant standards.

You can use the existing Object Store connector to write to the object store, regardless of whether
you are using v1 or v2.

The Object Store v2 user interface is available in the Anypoint Platform > Runtime Manager.

## Object Store v2 Permissions

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Toâ¦â</th><th>Required Permission</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Create, read, update, and delete stores.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Manage stores</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Perform all store operations including data, partition, and confirmation APIs.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Manage stores data</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Read store details.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View stores</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Manage all clients of a cloud store.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Manage store clients</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This permission doesnât apply to Object Store v2.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>View all clients of a cloud store.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View store clients (object store only)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This permission doesnât apply to Object Store v2.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Retrieve Object Store v2 metrics using the Object Store v2 Stats API.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Store Metrics Viewer</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

To assign user permissions, follow the steps in
[Grant Permissions to Users](../access-management/users#grant-user-permissions).

To use teams to assign permissions, follow the steps in
[Using Teams to Manage User Permissions](../access-management/teams).

## Time to Live (TTL)

The TTL can be either [_rolling_](#rolling-ttl) or [_static_](#static-ttl).

The `entryTtl` value, specified in the global configuration parameters for the connector, determines
when to evict key-value pairs from the store.

If the app doesnât specify a value for `entryTtl`, the default value depends on the Mule version for
the app:

- Mule versions 4.2.1 and later:

  Object stores have a rolling TTL by default.

  Accessing the data during the last seven days of a 30-day window extends the TTL for another 30
  days.

- Mule versions earlier than 4.2.1:

  Object stores have a static TTL of 30 days by default.

  Updating the data (for example, by overwriting the key) resets the TTL.

To configure TTL, see
[Configure a Custom Time to Live Period](osv2-guide#configure-a-custom-time-to-live-period).

### Rolling TTL

When you configure rolling TTL, accessing the data during the last seven days of a 30-day window
extends the TTL for another 30 days.

Rolling TTL applies to individual key-value pairs. Each key in a partition tracks its own TTL
window. Accessing one key doesnât extend the TTL for other keys in the same partition, and keys
expire independently based on their last access time.

If you donât access the data during that time, the object store evicts the data in the next seven to
30 days from the most recent expiration date.

The Object Store Connector operations that extend TTL are:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Extends TTL</th><th>Doesnât Extend TTL</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Retrieve</p></td><td><p>Contains</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retrieve All</p></td><td><p>Clear</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retrieve All Keys</p></td><td><p>Remove</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Store</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- The minimum rolling TTL is 30 days (2592000 seconds).
- The maximum rolling TTL is unlimited if you continue to access the data in the last seven days of
  the 30-day window.

### Static TTL

When you configure static TTL, the value of `entryTtl` determines when to evict the data:

- The minimum static TTL is one second.
- The maximum (and default) static TTL is 2592000 seconds (30 days).

If the `entryTtl` value is less than one second or greater than 30 days, TTL is set to the maximum
value of 2592000 seconds (30 days) without returning an error.

Updating the data (for example, by overwriting the key) resets the TTL.

## Upgrade to Object Store v2

- Back up all the data in your current object store.

  > [!IMPORTANT] Data doesnât carry over from Object Store v1 to v2. Any data in a v1 object store,
  > including watermarks and other Mule component states, doesnât move to v2. If you revert to
  > Object Store v1, your Object Store v1 data is still there.

- To access Object Store v2, in Anypoint Platform, click **Runtime Manager**. Click the name of your
  application to view the applicationâs dashboard, and click **Manage Application**. In the
  **Settings** screen, click **Use Object Store v2**. Accept the prompt. The **Application Data**
  section of the Runtime Managerâs dashboard changes to **Object Store**.

## Object Store Notes

- For Object Store v2 region availability see
  [Where is Object Store v2 available?](osv2-faq#where-is-object-store-v2-available)
- The Object Store Connector works for both Object Store version 1 and 2.
- Object Store v2 enforces a maximum data persistence of 30 days from time of creation.
- Premium add-on customers are allowed up to 100 TPS per app.

  The free Object Store v2 limits usage to 10 transactions per second (TPS) per application. To
  upgrade to premium and increase your TPS limit, contact the MuleSoft account team.

- For CloudHub applications using Mule 4, if you choose Object Store v2, Object Store v2 overrides
  the `_defaultPersistentObjectStore` object store.
- Using Object Store v2 with multi-worker CloudHub applications might result in data discrepancies
  or key clashes.

  This behavior occurs because the "Distributed Locking" feature isnât available in CloudHub and
  CloudHub 2.0 when working with Object Store v2. As a result, under certain conditions, when
  multiple Mule components attempt to update Object Store v2 tables simultaneously, such calls may
  fail.

  To avoid these issues, use a distributed key-value store as a lock to synchronize access to Object
  Store v2. For information about distributed locking in Mule runtime engine, see
  [Distributed Locking](../mule-runtime/latest/distributed-locking).

## Object Store Features by Control Plane

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Object Store Feature</th><th>US Cloud and EU Cloud</th><th>Canada Cloud and Japan Cloud (Hyperforce)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p></p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="osv2-connected-apps">Connected Apps</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p></p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="osv2-usage#access-usage-charts">Usage Charts</a> (Access Management)</p></li><li><p><a href="../cloudhub/deploying-to-cloudhub">CloudHub 1.0 Deployments</a></p></li><li><p><a href="osv2-apis#osv2-stats-api">Object Store v2 Stats API</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p></p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="osv2-usage#access-os-usage-reports">Usage Reports</a> (Usage)</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Object Store v2 on Hyperforce

Object Store v2 is available on these instances of Anypoint Platform:

- [Canada Cloud](https://ca1.platform.mulesoft.com)
- [Japan Cloud](https://jp1.platform.mulesoft.com)

For information about the regions where Object Store v2 is available, see
[Object Store v2 Regions](osv2-faq#osv2-regions).

When you deploy to Canada Cloud or Japan Cloud, all features of Object Store v2 are supported on
Hyperforce with these exceptions:

CloudHub 1

Deployments to CloudHub 1 arenât supported.

You can deploy apps to [CloudHub 2.0 shared spaces](../cloudhub-2/ch2-shared-space-about) and
[Anypoint Runtime Fabric](../runtime-fabric/latest/).

Usage Charts

[Usage Charts](osv2-usage#access-usage-charts) in Access Management arenât available. Use
[Usage Reports](osv2-usage#access-os-usage-reports) in Usage instead.

REST APIs

The [Object Store v2 Stats API](osv2-apis#osv2-stats-api) isnât supported.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Hyperforce Overview](../hyperforce/)
- [Permissions by Product in Anypoint Platform](../access-management/permissions-by-product)
- [Object Store v2 Release Notes](../release-notes/object-store/anypoint-osv2-release-notes)
- [Object Store Connector for Mule 4](../object-store-connector/latest/)
- [Object Store V2 Stats API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/object-store-v2-stats/)
- [Object Store Watermarks](https://blogs.mulesoft.com/dev-guides/how-to-tutorials/data-synchronizing-made-easy-with-mule-watermarks/)
---

# Object Store v2 REST APIs

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

The Object Store v2 APIs enable you to use REST to:

- Retrieve a list of object stores and keys associated with an application.
- Store and retrieve key-value pairs in an object store.
- Delete key-value pairs from an object store.
- Retrieve Object Store usage statistics for your organization.

Object Store provides these APIs:

- [Object Store v2 API](#osv2-api)
- [Object Store v2 Stats API](#osv2-stats-api)

## Before You Begin

- Create an app that uses Object Store v2 and
  [deploy it to CloudHub](../cloudhub/deploying-to-cloudhub).
- [Store a key-value pair in the object store](osv2-guide#send-data-to-an-object-store).
- [Get the bearer token](#get-bearer-token) for an Anypoint Platform user using the Access
  Management API:

  You need the bearer token to access all Object Store v2 APIs.

- [Get the ORG_ID and ENV_ID](#get-org-env-id).

  You need the organization ID and environment ID for operations with all Object Store v2 APIs.

- [Get the REGION_ID](#get-region-id).

  You need the region ID for operations with the Object Store v2 Stats API.

- [Get the STORE_ID](#get-store-id).

  You need the store ID for operations with the Object Store v2 Stats API.

## Get a Bearer Token to Use with the Object Store v2 APIs

If MFA (multi-factor authentication) is enabled

Use a connected app to get the bearer token:

1.  [Configure a connected app](osv2-connected-apps).

    Assign the connected app the Organization Administrator scope to access the organization and
    environment queries.

2.  From the **Connected Apps** page, click **Copy Id** and **Copy Secret** for the app you just
    created.

    Use this client ID and client secret to get the bearer token required to authenticate with the
    Object Store v2 API.

3.  Run this command to get the bearer token from the connected app client ID and client secret:

    ```bash
    curl --location '<AUTH_SERVER_HOST>/accounts/api/v2/oauth2/token' \
       --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        --data '{
          "client_id": "<CLIENT_ID>",
          "client_secret": "<CLIENT_SECRET>",
          "grant_type": "client_credentials"
          }'
    ```

    Replace AUTH_SERVER_HOST with the URL of the authorization server, for example
    `https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/`.

    The output includes the bearer token:

    ```json
    {
      "access_token": "<BEARER_TOKEN>",
      "token_type": "bearer"
    }
    ```

4.  Use this bearer token to access any Object Store v2 API that requires a bearer token.

If MFA isnât enabled

Authenticate with the credentials of a user with the Organization Administrator permission:

1.  Run this command to get the bearer token:

    ```bash
    curl -H \
      "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -X POST -d '{"username":"<USERNAME>","password":"<PASSWORD>"}' \
      "<AUTH_SERVER_HOST>accounts/login"
    ```

    Replace AUTH_SERVER_HOST with the URL of the authorization server, for example
    `https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/`.

    Make sure the user has the Organization Administrator permission to access the organization and
    environment queries.

    The output includes the bearer token:

    ```json
    {
      "access_token": "<BEARER_TOKEN>",
      "expires_in": 3600,
      "token_type": "bearer"
    }
    ```

2.  Use this bearer token to access any Object Store v2 API that requires a bearer token.

### Get the Organization ID and Environment ID

To get the ORG_ID, run this command, replacing AUTH_SERVER_HOST with the authorization server:

```bash
curl -X GET https://<AUTH_SERVER_HOST>/accounts/api/me \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>"
```

The response includes output similar to:

```json
"organization": {
    "name": "MyCompany",
    "id": "ORG_ID",
  },
```

To get the ENV_ID, run this command, replacing AUTH_SERVER_HOST with the authorization server:

```bash
curl -X GET https://<AUTH_SERVER_HOST>/accounts/api/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>"
```

The response includes output similar to:

```json
"environments": [
  {
    "id": "ENV_ID",
    "name": "Production",
    "organizationId": "ORG_ID",
  },
]
```

### Get the Region ID

To get the region ID (REGION_ID), run this command, replacing [BASE_URL](#access-osv2-api):

```bash
curl -X GET \
 'https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/regions' \
 -H 'authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>'
```

See [Example: Retrieve a List of Regions](#example-retrieve-region).

### Get the Store ID

To get the store ID (STORE_ID), run this command, replacing [BASE_URL](#access-osv2-api):

```bash
curl -X GET \
 'https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/stores' \
 -H 'authorization: bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>'
```

See [Example: Retrieve a List of Object Stores](#example-retrieve-list).

## Object Store v2 API

The Object Store v2 API reads and writes `key,list` values, which is different from Anypoint
Connector for Object Store (Object Store Connector), which reads and writes in the `key,value`
format.

This table compares the Object Store REST API operations to Object Store Connector operations:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Object Store v2 API</th><th>Object Store v2 REST API Operations</th><th>Object Store Connector Operation</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>POST</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Object Store</p><div><ul><li><p>Object store TTL, in seconds</p><div><p>The maximum TTL is 2592000 seconds (30 days).</p></div><div><p>If the <code>entryTtl</code> value specified is greater than 30 days, the value defaults to 2592000 seconds (30 days), without returning an error.</p></div></li><li><p>Persistence property</p></li><li><p>Usage limits for each application or environment, including options for limits based on keys or size, and how to enforce them</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Key and list (item) value</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Store</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>GET</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Get keys</p></li><li><p>Get item</p></li><li><p>Get items</p></li><li><p>Get all items</p></li><li><p>Get regions for an organization</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Contains + Retrieve + Retrieve all keys + Retrieve and store (Retrieve part)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>PUT</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Put item as string</p></li><li><p>Put item as number</p></li><li><p>Put item as confirmable string</p></li><li><p>Put store</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Store</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>DELETE</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Remove object store</p></li><li><p>Delete key</p></li><li><p>Delete item</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Remove</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

You can retrieve a maximum of 25 keys with each GET operation.

### Access the Object Store v2 API

1.  [Get a Bearer Token to Use with the Object Store v2 APIs](#get-bearer-token).
2.  Set up an application, such as Postman, to access the object store.
3.  Configure the object store access URL, replacing ORG_ID with your organization ID and ENV_ID
    with your environment ID:

    ```bash
    <BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/stores/myTestStoreId/objects
    ```

    Depending on the deployment region, use one of these domains for the value of BASE_URL in the
    access URL:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Control Plane</th><th>BASE_URL</th><th>Region</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td rowspan="12"><p>U.S.</p></td><td><div><div><p><code>object-store-us-east-1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>US East (N. Virginia)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-us-east-2.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>US East (Ohio)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-us-west-1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>US West (N. California)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-us-west-2.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>US West (Oregon)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-ap-southeast-1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Asia Pacific (Singapore)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-ap-southeast-2.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Asia Pacific (Sydney)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-ap-northeast-1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Asia Pacific (Tokyo)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-ca-central-1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Canada (Central)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-eu-central-1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Europe (Frankfurt)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-eu-west-1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Europe (Ireland)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-eu-west-2.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>UK (London)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-sa-east-1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>South America (SÃ£o Paulo)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>U.S.</p><p>MuleSoft Government Cloud</p></td><td><div><div><p><code>object-store-us-gov-west-1.gov.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>US Gov West</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>EU</p></td><td><div><div><p><code>object-store-eu-central-1.eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Europe (Frankfurt)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>object-store-eu-west-1.eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Europe (Ireland)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Canada Cloud</p></td><td><div><div><p><code>object-store-ca-central-1.ca1.platform.mulesoft.com</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Canada (Central)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>Japan Cloud</p></td><td><div><div><p><code>object-store-ap-northeast-1.jp1.platform.mulesoft.com</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Asia Pacific (Tokyo)</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  Configure the application with HTTP headers and body for values to store to or read from the
    object store.
5.  Send the operation to the Object Store v2 API.

### Examples: Object Store v2 API

- [Example: Retrieve a List of Object Stores](#example-retrieve-list)
- [Example: Retrieve a List of Regions](#example-retrieve-region)
- [Example: Retrieve a List of Partitions](#example-retrieve-partitions)
- [Example: Retrieve a List of Keys in a Partition](#example-retrieve-keys)
- [Example: Retrieve the Value of a Key](#example-retrieve-key-value)
- [Example: Store a Key-Value Pair](#example-store-key-value)
- [Example: Update a Key-Value Pair](#example-update-key-value)
- [Example: Delete a Key and Value](#example-delete-key-value)

#### Example: Retrieve a List of Object Stores

To view the list of object stores, run a `curl` command, replacing BASE_URL with the regional
domain, ORG_ID with your organization ID, ENV_ID with your environment ID, and BEARER_TOKEN with
your bearer token:

```bash
curl -X GET \
 'https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/stores' \
 -H 'authorization: bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>'
```

This command returns output similar to:

```json
  {"values":[{"storeId":"APP_os-simple-flow__defaultPersistentObjectStore","encrypted":true,"permanentOsFlag":false,
    "persistent":true,"defaultTtlSeconds":2592000,"defaultConfirmationTtlSeconds":600,"isFixedTtl":null},
  {"storeId":"APP_scheduler-tests__defaultPersistentObjectStore","encrypted":true,"permanentOsFlag":false,
    "persistent":true,"defaultTtlSeconds":2592000,"defaultConfirmationTtlSeconds":600,"isFixedTtl":null}
  {"storeId":"APP_sending-a-csv-file-thru-email-using-smtp__defaultPersistentObjectStore","encrypted":true,"permanentOsFlag":false,
    "persistent":true,"defaultTtlSeconds":2592000,"defaultConfirmationTtlSeconds":600,"isFixedTtl":null}],"nextPageToken":null}
```

#### Example: Retrieve a List of Regions

To view the list of regions, submit a `curl` command, replacing BASE_URL with the regional domain,
ORG_ID with your organization ID, and BEARER_TOKEN with your bearer token:

```bash
curl -X GET \
 'https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/regions' \
 -H 'authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>'
```

This command returns output similar to:

```json
[
  {
    "id": "us-east-1",
    "name": "US East (N. Virginia)",
    "url": "http://object-store.us-east-1.anypoint.mulesoft.com"
  },
  {
    "id": "us-west-1",
    "name": "US West (N. California)",
    "url": "http://object-store.us-west-1.anypoint.mulesoft.com"
  }
]
```

#### Example: Retrieve a List of Partitions

To view the list of partitions, submit a `curl` command, replacing BASE_URL with the regional
domain, ORG_ID with your organization ID, ENV_ID with your environment ID, and STORE_ID with the
`storeId` from the list of object stores, and BEARER_TOKEN with your bearer token:

```bash
curl -X GET \
  https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/stores/<STORE_ID>/partitions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>"
```

This command returns output similar to:

```json
{ "values": ["PARTITION_ID"], "nextPageToken": null }
```

#### Example: Retrieve a List of Keys in a Partition

To view the keys in a partition, submit a `curl` command, replacing BASE_URL with the regional
domain, ORG_ID with your organization ID, ENV_ID with your environment ID, STORE_ID with the name of
the object store, PARTITION_ID with the name of the partition, and BEARER_TOKEN with your bearer
token:

```bash
curl -X GET \
  https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/stores/<STORE_ID>/partitions/<PARTITION_ID>/keys \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>"
```

This command returns output similar to:

```json
{ "values": [{ "keyId": "KeyTwo" }, { "keyId": "KeyOne" }], "nextPageToken": null }
```

#### Example: Retrieve the Value of a Key

To view the value of a key, submit a `curl` command, replacing BASE_URL with the regional domain,
ORG_ID with your organization ID, ENV_ID with your environment ID, STORE_ID with the name of the
object store, PARTITION_ID with the name of the partition, KEY_ID with the key ID, and BEARER_TOKEN
with your bearer token:

```bash
curl -X GET \
  https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/stores/<STORE_ID>/partitions/<PARTITION_ID>/keys/<KEY_ID> \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>"
```

This command returns output similar to:

```json
{
  "binaryValue": "++Kn0AIBAQBbwoABDiJLZXkgMiBWYWx1ZSL8rNmhAQEBAWphdmEuaW8uU2VyaWFsaXphYmzlgGFwcGxpY2F0aW9uL2pzb247IGNoYXJzZXQ9VVRGLbgAAAAAAAAADQ==",
  "keyId": "KeyTwo",
  "valueType": "BINARY"
}
```

#### Example: Store a Key-Value Pair

To store a key-value pair, submit a `curl` command, replacing BASE_URL with the regional domain,
ORG_ID with your organization ID, ENV_ID with your environment ID, STORE_ID with the name of the
object store, PARTITION_ID with the name of the partition, BEARER_TOKEN with your bearer token, and
specify a value (`myTestKey`) for KEY_ID:

```bash
KEY_ID=myTestKey; \
curl -X POST \
  https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/stores/<STORE_ID>/partitions/<PARTITION_ID>/keys/myTestKey \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{"stringValue":"Hello World","keyId":"myTestKey","valueType":"STRING"}'
```

In this command, the value is a JSON string.

To verify that the key-pair is in the object store, get a list of the keys in the partition:

```bash
curl -X GET \
  https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/stores/<STORE_ID>/partitions/<PARTITION_ID>/keys \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>"
```

This command returns output similar to:

```json
{
  "values": [{ "keyId": "KeyTwo" }, { "keyId": "KeyOne" }, { "keyId": "myTestKey" }],
  "nextPageToken": null
}
```

#### Example: Update a Key-Value Pair

To update the value of an existing key or create a new one, use a `PUT` request. Replace the
placeholders with your specific values:

```bash
curl -X PUT \
  'https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/stores/<STORE_ID>/partitions/<PARTITION_ID>/keys/<KEY_ID>' \
  -H 'authorization: bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>' \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{
        "stringValue": "Updated value",
        "valueType": "STRING",
        "metadata": {
          "source": "api"
        }
      }'
```

This command returns output similar to:

```json
{
  "stringValue": "Updated value",
  "valueType": "STRING",
  "metadata": {
    "source": "api"
  }
}
```

#### Example: Delete a Key and Value

To delete a key-value pair, submit a `curl` command, replacing BASE_URL with the regional domain,
ORG_ID with your organization ID, ENV_ID with your environment ID, STORE_ID with the name of the
object store, PARTITION_ID with the name of the partition, KEY_ID with the key ID, and BEARER_TOKEN
with your bearer token:

```bash
curl -X DELETE \
  https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/stores/<STORE_ID>/partitions/<PARTITION_ID>/keys/KEY_ID \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>"
```

In this command, the value is a JSON string.

To verify that the key-pair is no longer in the object store, get a list of the keys in the
partition:

```bash
curl -X GET \
  https://<BASE_URL>/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/stores/<STORE_ID>/partitions/<PARTITION_ID>/keys \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>"
```

This command returns output similar to:

```json
{ "values": [{ "keyId": "KeyTwo" }, { "keyId": "KeyOne" }], "nextPageToken": null }
```

## Object Store v2 Stats API

> [!NOTE] Only the US Cloud and EU Cloud instances of Anypoint Platform support these REST APIs.

The Object Store v2 Stats API enables you to retrieve statistics on your Object Store usage.

The usage statistics arenât real-time and might include latency.

Use the Stats API to view your entire organizationâs usage of Object Store for billing purposes:

- Usage per root organization and all sub-organizations
- Usage per organization
- Usage per environment
- Usage per region
- Usage per store

### Usage Metrics Limits

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Values</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Time series (<code>period</code>)</p></td><td><p>Interval that the query records</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><code>1hour</code></p></li><li><p><code>1day</code></p></li><li><p><code>1month</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Data retention</p></td><td><p>Length of time that Object Store retains the data, based on <code>period</code></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><code>period=1hour</code>: 3 months</p></li><li><p><code>period=1day</code>: 12 months</p></li><li><p><code>period=1month</code>: 24 months</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query timeframe</p></td><td><p>Maximum interval between <code>startDate</code> and <code>endDate</code> in the query, based on <code>period</code></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><code>period=1hour</code>: 1 day</p></li><li><p><code>period=1day</code>: 1 month</p></li><li><p><code>period=1month</code>: 2 months</p></li></ul></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">If the interval between <code>startDate</code> and <code>endDate</code> exceeds the limits, the API returns an HTTP 400 error.</div></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Access the Object Store v2 Stats API

1.  [Get a Bearer Token to Use with the Object Store v2 APIs](#get-bearer-token).
2.  Set up an application, such as Postman, to access the object store.
3.  Configure the access URL, replacing ORG_ID with your organization ID and ENV_ID with your
    environment ID:

    ```bash
    https://object-store-stats.anypoint.mulesoft.com/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/ENV_ID?startDate=...&endDate=...&period=...
    ```

    > [!IMPORTANT] The query parameters must be URL-encoded to preserve their values.

For example, you must URL-encode a `startDate` of `2024-07-11T17:51:54.000Z` to
`11T17%3A51%3A54.000Z` before sending it in the query. . Configure the application with HTTP headers
and body for values to store to or read from the object store. . Send the operation to the Object
Store v2 Stats API.

See also
[How to use Object Store v2 Stats API](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=002778591&type=1).

### Examples: Object Store v2 Stats API

- [Example: Get Usage Metrics by Organization](#usage-by-org)
- [Example: Get Usage Metrics by Environment](#usage-by-env)
- [Example: Get Usage Metrics by Region](#usage-by-region)
- [Example: Get Usage Metrics by Store](#usage-by-store)

#### Example: Get Usage Metrics by Organization

By default, the Object Store v2 Stats API endpoint retrieves statistics only for the specified
ORG_ID. To get the usage data for the root organization and all sub-organizations, set `isMaster` to
`true` when ORG_ID is the root organization. If you use `isMaster=true` to query a sub-organization,
the endpoint returns no results.

To view Object Store usage statistics for the root organization and all sub-organizations, between
August 11, 2024 and September 11, 2024, submit a `curl` command, replacing ORG_ID with the
organization ID for your root organization and BEARER_TOKEN with your bearer token:

```bash
curl -X GET \
  'https://object-store-stats.anypoint.mulesoft.com/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>?startDate=2024-08-11T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&endDate=2024-09-11T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&period=1month&isMaster=true' \
  -H 'authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>'
```

This request returns a response similar to:

```json
[
  { "timeStamp": "2024-08-01T00:00:00Z", "objectStoreRequestCount": 38 },
  { "timeStamp": "2024-09-01T00:00:00Z", "objectStoreRequestCount": 28 }
]
```

To view Object Store usage statistics for the root organization and all sub-organizations for one
day, submit a `curl` command, replacing ORG_ID with the organization ID for your root organization
and BEARER_TOKEN with your bearer token:

```bash
curl -X GET \
  'https://object-store-stats.anypoint.mulesoft.com/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>?startDate=2024-11-11T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&endDate=2024-11-131T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&period=1day&isMaster=true' \
  -H 'authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>'
```

This request returns a response similar to:

```json
[
 {"timeStamp":"2024-11-11T00:00:00Z","objectStoreRequestCount":36}
 {"timeStamp":"2024-11-12T00:00:00Z","objectStoreRequestCount":22}
 {"timeStamp":"2024-11-13T00:00:00Z","objectStoreRequestCount":43}
]
```

To view Object Store usage statistics for a sub-organization, between February 10, 2024 and March
10, 2024, submit a `curl` command, replacing ORG_ID with the organization ID for the
sub-organization and BEARER_TOKEN with your bearer token:

```bash
curl -X GET \
  'https://object-store-stats.anypoint.mulesoft.com/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>?startDate=2024-02-10T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&endDate=2024-03-10T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&period=1month' \
  -H 'authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>'
```

This request returns a response similar to:

```json
[
  { "timeStamp": "2024-02-10T00:00:00Z", "objectStoreRequestCount": 1 },
  { "timeStamp": "2024-03-10T00:00:00Z", "objectStoreRequestCount": 15 }
]
```

To view Object Store usage statistics for one day for a sub-organization, submit a `curl` command,
replacing ORG_ID with the organization ID for the sub-organization and BEARER_TOKEN with your bearer
token:

```bash
curl -X GET \
  'https://object-store-stats.anypoint.mulesoft.com/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>?startDate=2024-12-01T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&endDate=2024-12-03T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&period=1day' \
  -H 'authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>'
```

This request returns a response similar to:

```json
[
 {"timeStamp":"2024-12-01T00:00:00Z","objectStoreRequestCount":4}
 {"timeStamp":"2024-12-02T00:00:00Z","objectStoreRequestCount":2}
 {"timeStamp":"2024-12-03T00:00:00Z","objectStoreRequestCount":8}

]
```

#### Example: Get Usage Metrics by Environment

To view Object Store usage statistics for an environment between August 13, 2024 and September 13,
2024, submit a `curl` command, replacing ORG_ID with your organization ID, ENV_ID with your
environment ID, and BEARER_TOKEN with your bearer token:

```bash
curl -X GET \
  'https://object-store-stats.anypoint.mulesoft.com/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>?startDate=2024-08-13T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&endDate=2024-09-13T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&period=1month' \
  -H 'authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>'
```

This request returns a response similar to:

```json
[
  { "timeStamp": "2024-08-01T00:00:00Z", "objectStoreRequestCount": 1 },
  { "timeStamp": "2024-09-01T00:00:00Z", "objectStoreRequestCount": 25 }
]
```

#### Example: Get Usage Metrics by Region

To view Object Store usage statistics for a region, submit a `curl` command, replacing ORG_ID with
your organization ID, ENV_ID with your environment ID, REGION_ID with the region from which to
retrieve metrics, and BEARER_TOKEN with your bearer token:

```bash
 curl -X GET \
  'https://object-store-stats.anypoint.mulesoft.com/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/regions/<REGION_ID>?startDate=2024-08-13T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&endDate=2024-09-13T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&period=1month' \
  -H 'authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>'
```

To retrieve the REGION_ID, see [Example: Retrieve a List of Regions](#example-retrieve-region).

This request returns a response similar to:

```json
[
  { "timeStamp": "2024-08-01T00:00:00Z", "objectStoreRequestCount": 1 },
  { "timeStamp": "2024-09-01T00:00:00Z", "objectStoreRequestCount": 25 }
]
```

#### Example: Get Usage Metrics by Store

To view Object Store usage statistics for a store, submit a `curl` command, replacing ORG_ID with
your organization ID, ENV_ID with your environment ID, REGION_ID with the region to retrieve metrics
from, STORE_ID with the store ID to retrieve metrics for, and BEARER_TOKEN with your bearer token:

```bash
 curl -X GET \
  'https://object-store-stats.anypoint.mulesoft.com/api/v1/organizations/<ORG_ID>/environments/<ENV_ID>/regions/<REGION_ID>/stores/<STORE_ID>?startDate=2024-08-13T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&endDate=2024-09-13T17%3A51%3A54.000Z&period=1month' \
  -H 'authorization: Bearer <BEARER_TOKEN>'
```

To retrieve the STORE_ID, see [Example: Retrieve a List of Object Stores](#example-retrieve-list).

This request returns a response similar to:

```json
[
  { "timeStamp": "2024-08-01T00:00:00Z", "objectStoreRequestCount": 1 },
  { "timeStamp": "2024-09-01T00:00:00Z", "objectStoreRequestCount": 25 }
]
```

## Download Object Store APIs from Exchange

To download the Object Store APIs from Exchange:

1.  Go to Exchange and click **Download**.
2.  Select the format to download:
    - RAML
    - OAS
    - Mule 4 connector file

![Download button for the Object Store v2 API in Exchange](_images/os-exchange-download.png)

## See Also

- [Rolling TTL](./#rolling-ttl)
- [Object Store v2 API in Anypoint Exchange](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/object-store-v2/)
- [Object Store v2 Stats API in Anypoint Exchange](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/object-store-v2-stats/)
- [Access Management API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/access-management-api/)
- [Object Store Connector for Mule 4](../object-store-connector/latest/)
---

# Configuring Connected Apps

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The Connected Apps feature enables you to integrate external applications with Anypoint Platform by
providing access to those applications without sharing your user credentials.

For information about Connected Apps, see
[Connected Apps in Anypoint Platform](../access-management/connected-apps-overview) and
[Anypoint Platform Connected Apps for Developers](../access-management/connected-apps-developers).

## Configure a Connected App in Access Management

To create a Connected App with object store scopes:

1.  In Anypoint Platform, go to **Access Management**.
2.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click **Connected Apps**.
3.  Click **Create App**.
    1.  Specify a name for the app.
    2.  Select **App acts on its own behalf (client credentials)**.
    3.  Click **Add Scopes**.
    4.  Select the object store scopes to apply:

        ![Scopes for Object Store connected apps](_images/os-conn-apps-scopes.png)

    5.  Click **Next** and select the business groups and environments to apply the scopes to.
    6.  Click **Review**.
    7.  Click **Add scopes** or **Cancel** to cancel your changes.

4.  Click **Save** or **Cancel** to cancel your changes.
5.  From the **Connected Apps** page, click **Copy Id** and **Copy Secret** for the app you just
    created.

    Use this client ID and client secret to get the access token required to authenticate with the
    Object Store v2 API.

## Configure a Connected App Using the API

For information about configuring a connected app using the API, see
[How to create connected apps using the API](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=001115949&type=1).

## Object Store Scopes

Object store scopes apply to all stores in the given organization and environment. Unless otherwise
noted, these scopes apply to both object stores and Object Store v2 object stores.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Scope in UI</th><th>Scope in API</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Manage stores</p></td><td><p><code>manage:store</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Create, read, update, and delete stores.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Manage stores data</p></td><td><p><code>manage:store_data</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Perform all store operations including data, partition, and confirmation APIs.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View stores</p></td><td><p><code>read:store</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Read store details.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Manage store clients (object store only)</p></td><td><p>Not Available</p></td><td><div><div><p>Manage all clients of a cloud store.</p></div><div><p>This scope doesnât apply to Object Store v2.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View store clients (object store only)</p></td><td><p>Not Available</p></td><td><div><div><p>View all clients of a cloud store.</p></div><div><p>This scope doesnât apply to Object Store v2.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Store Metrics Viewer</p></td><td><p><code>read:store_metrics</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Retrieve Object Store v2 metrics using the Object Store v2 Stats API.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# FAQ: Object Store v2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Where is Object Store v2 available?

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Location</th><th>Region Name</th><th>Region</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><th colspan="3"><p><a href="https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/">US Cloud</a></p></th></tr><tr><td rowspan="5"><div><div><p>North America</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>US East (N. Virginia)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>us-east-1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>US East (Ohio)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>us-east-2</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>US West (N. California)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>us-west-1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>US West (Oregon)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>us-west-2</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Canada (Central)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>ca-central-1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>South America</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>South America (Sao Paulo)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>sa-east-1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><div><div><p>APAC</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Asia Pacific (Singapore)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>ap-southeast-1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Asia Pacific (Sydney)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>ap-southeast-2</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Asia Pacific (Tokyo)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>ap-northeast-1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><div><div><p>Europe</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Europe (Ireland)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>eu-west-1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>EU (Frankfurt)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>eu-central-1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>UK (London)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>eu-west-2</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><th colspan="3"><p>MuleSoft Government Cloud</p></th></tr><tr><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>GovCloud (US West)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>us-gov-west-1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><th colspan="3"><p><a href="https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/">EU Cloud</a></p></th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><div><div><p>Europe</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>EU (Ireland)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>eu-west-1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>EU (Frankfurt)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>eu-central-1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><th colspan="3"><p><a href="https://ca1.platform.mulesoft.com">Canada Cloud</a></p></th></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>North America</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Canada (Central)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>ca-central-1</code> <a href="#os-hyperforce"><sup><strong>*</strong></sup></a></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><th colspan="3"><p><a href="https://jp1.platform.mulesoft.com">Japan Cloud</a></p></th></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>APAC</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Japan (Central)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>ap-northeast-1</code> <a href="#os-hyperforce"><sup><strong>*</strong></sup></a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

**\*** You canât access or configure some Object Store v2 features, such as the Object Store v2
Stats API. For information, see [Object Store v2 on Hyperforce](./#osv2-on-hyperforce).

## What Mule versions support Object Store v2?

- All Mule runtime engine 4.x versions

  Mule 4 supports Object Store v2, but does not support Object Store v1.

## Can I use Object Store v2 for apps deployed to on-premises servers?

On-premises Mule applications can access Object Store v2 only via the Object Store REST API.

For on-premises deployments, you can use the object store included in Mule. For information about
the Mule object store, see
[Store Application Data Using Object Stores](../mule-runtime/latest/mule-object-stores).

## How can I see usage data?

To view API request data, see [View Usage Graphs](osv2-usage).

## What happens when the transaction limit for my subscription is reached?

An Object Store v2 subscription pack allows up to 100 million transactions per month for an Anypoint
Platform organization. This limit is applied across all Production and non-Production environments.
You can use the
[Object Store v2 Stats API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/object-store-v2-stats/)
to monitor usage.

If your usage exceeds your license limit, Object Store v2 continues to work. MuleSoft notifies your
account administrator and team of any billing-related information.

## Is Object Store v2 persistent in the same region as the worker?

Yes, Object Store v2 is in the same region as the worker where the app is initially deployed. For
example, if you deploy to the Singapore region, the object store persists in the Singapore region.

> [!IMPORTANT] If after the first deploy, you move the app to a different region, Object Store v2
> remains in the original region to avoid data loss. Your use of Object Store v2 never moves from
> one region to another. When you need to deploy both an app and object store to a different region,
> delete the app and re-upload the app into the new region. This action loses all the data that may
> exist in the storage.

## Can an app deployed to CloudHub access the object store of another CloudHub app?

You can configure a Mule app to use the Object Store REST API to store and retrieve values from an
object store in another Mule app.

However, Object Store v2 isnât designed for app-to-app communication. To share data between two Mule
4 apps, use a queue in [Anypoint MQ](../mq/).

For information about object store limitations, see
[Object Store Limitations](../object-store-connector/latest/#object-store-limitations).

For information about the Object Store REST API, see
[Object Store v2 API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/object-store-v2/)
on Exchange.

## What are the limits on an object store?

The total size of an object store isnât limited, but each value is limited to 10 MB.

Every API call (via the connector or API) to Object Store v2 counts toward the API rate limiting
(transactions per second (TPS)):

- Base subscription: 10 TPS per app
- Premium add-on subscription: 100 TPS per app

## What is the maximum number of keys that can persist in Object Store v2?

In Object Store v2, there is no limit on the number of keys per app.

## What is the maximum key size?

The maximum size for a key is 1024 bytes (UTF-8 encoding).

Object Store v2 for CloudHub doesnât support using pipe (`|`) characters in keys and converts any
spaces to plus (`+`) characters.

## What are the advantages of using partitions?

You can use partitions to divide the object store into separate areas for your stored and retrieved
keys. Partitions are logical, rather than physical, structures. For this reason, data access time is
the same, whether you store keys in one partition or multiple partitions.

## How long can data persist in Object Store v2?

The maximum TTL (time to live) is 2592000 seconds (30 days).

For information about rolling and static TTL, see [Time to Live (TTL)](./#time-to-live).

To configure TTL, see
[Configure a Custom Time to Live Period](osv2-guide#configure-a-custom-time-to-live-period).

## Where is the Object Store v2 REST API portal?

The Object Store v2 APIs are available in the Anypoint Platform Developerâs portal:

- [Anypoint Exchange Object Store v2](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/object-store-v2/)
- [Anypoint Exchange Object Store v2 Stats](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/object-store-v2-stats/)

## Where is the Object Store connector?

[Anypoint Exchange](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/?search=objectstore) provides assets for
the Mule 4 Object Store connector used by Studio 7.

## Do I need a different Object Store connector for Object Store v2 and Object Store v1?

All versions of Muleâs Object Store Connector work with both Object Store v1 and Object Store v2.

## Why are Object Store values in binary format?

When you view an Object Store key in Runtime Manager, the value for the key displays as
`[binary value] BINARY`. Mule 4 wraps values into a Mule object that causes them to be only visible
in binary through Anypoint Platform. Behind the scenes, Mule 4 executes binary serialization with
the Mule internal serializer. The user interface canât deserialize the object, hence it can only
tell you that the value is now binary in the user interface.

To view the key value, you need an Object Store retrieve flow in your appâs connector, such as:

```xml
<flow name="Retrieve" >
	<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config1"
		path="/retrieve">
		<ee:repeatable-file-store-stream />
	</http:listener>
	<os:retrieve doc:name="Retrieve"
		key="#[attributes.queryParams.key]"
		objectStore="Object_store1"/>
</flow>
```

For more information, see
[Tutorial: Create a Studio 7 Project](osv2-tutorial#create-a-studio-7-project).

## Are there rate limits on Object Store v2?

Customers who have not purchased a separate Object Store SKU are rate limited to 10 transactions per
second per app. If an app for an account that has not purchased the Object Store v2 SKU exceeds the
rate limit, further requests arenât processed for a time and a 429 HTTP error status code results.
To go beyond the base 10 TPS rate limit, contact your MuleSoft account team.

## How is Object Store v2 data secured?

Object Store v2 uses Transport Layer Security (TLS) 1.2 and 1.3 for secure transport and doesnât
support the deprecated TLS versions 1.0 and 1.1. Data at rest is stored using FIPS 140-2 compliant
encryption standards. If you require a higher level of security, we recommend that you encrypt
sensitive data before writing it to the Object Store.

## Does Object Store cache data or perform I/O for each read and write?

Object Store v2 performs I/O for each read and write. With Object Store v2, the API call is
localized to the same data center as the Runtime Manager app.

## What happens when you reenable the **Use Object Store v2** option after disabling it?

By default, the **Use Object Store v2** option is enabled during CloudHub application deployment. If
you disable and then reenable this option in Runtime Manager, the original key-value pairs persist
as long as the key-value pair TTL isnât expired.

## See Also

- [Anypoint Exchange Object Store v2 API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/object-store-v2/)
- [Anypoint Exchange Object Store v2 Stats API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/object-store-v2-stats/)
- [Configure a Custom Time-to-Live Period](osv2-guide#configure-a-custom-time-to-live-period)
- [Create Object Store Partitions](osv2-guide#create-object-store-partitions)
- [Object Store Connector for Mule 4](../object-store-connector/latest/)
- [The Different Types of Object Stores Explained](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=001123185&type=1)
---

# Using Object Store v2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Object Store v2 (Object Store v2) enables you to share data and states across Mule runtime
workers within a single application.

Object Store v2 is enabled by default in Mule 4 and in Anypoint Platform EU Control Plane.

## Components

Object Store v2 interacts with these components:

- Anypoint Connector for Object Store (Object Store Connector)

  Use Object Store Connector with Anypoint Studio to construct flows to write to and read from the
  object store.

  Object Store Connector works with both Object Store version 1 and version 2.

  To use Object Store v2 with Mule 4 apps, ensure that you select the correct object store
  configuration reference from the **Object store** menu.

- REST API

  The Object Store v2 API enables you to create and delete objects, set TTL (time to live) for
  objects, and set usage limits.

  The Object Store v2 Stats API enables you to retrieve Object Store v2 usage statistics, such as
  how many API calls per month your organization makes to Object Store.

- Anypoint Runtime Manager

  When you enable Object Store v2, the **Runtime Manager > Application Data** option changes to
  **Object Store**. From the **Object Store** page, you can manage objects and their values in the
  object store.

### Protect Object Store Data

Object Store v2 uses TLS for secure transport. Data at rest is stored using FIPS 140-2-compliant
encryption standards. If you require a higher level of security, encrypt sensitive data before
writing it to the object store.

### Synchronize Access to Object Store v2

Using Object Store v2 with multi-worker CloudHub applications might result in data discrepancies or
key clashes.

To avoid these issues, use a distributed key-value store as a lock to synchronize access to Object
Store v2. For information about distributed locking in Mule runtime engine, see
[Distributed Locking](../mule-runtime/latest/distributed-locking).

## Add Object Store Connector to Your Mule 4 Project

Install Object Store Connector to add it to a Mule project:

1.  In Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, enter `Object Store` in the search field.
4.  In **Available modules**, click **ObjectStore Connector - Mule 4** and click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

## Use Object Store Connector in an Application

1.  In Studio, create a simple flow such as:

    ![Flow in Studio showing the Listener and Store operations](_images/os-studio7-flow.png)

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Connector</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Parameters</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>HTTP Listener</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>Connector configuration</strong> = <code>HTTP_Listener_config</code></p></div><div><p><strong>Path</strong> = <code>/store</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>Key</strong> = <code>#[payload.key]</code></p></div><div><p><strong>Value</strong> = <code>[payload.value]</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

    Object Store v2 for CloudHub doesnât support using pipe (`|`) characters in keys and converts
    any spaces to plus (`+`) characters.

    > [!IMPORTANT] To use Object Store v2, ensure that the **Persistent** option is selected for the
    > object store configuration reference in the **Global Elements** page. If the XML includes
    > `persistent="false"`, the app does not use Object Store v2.

2.  Save the application.
3.  Right-click the application name in **Package Explorer** and select **Anypoint Platform > Deploy
    to CloudHub**.
4.  If prompted, sign in to Anypoint Platform.
5.  Click **Deploy Application** and click **Open in Browser**.
6.  In Runtime Manager, click the **Status** column for your application.
7.  In the details pane, copy the **App url** address.

## Send Data to an Object Store

From a command line prompt, send JSON data to your Mule application using a `curl` command:

curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{ "key": "TestKey01", "value": "Testing" }'
"http://app-url/store"

Change `app-url` to the name you copied from **App url** in Runtime Manager.

## View Data in an Object Store

To view data in an object store:

1.  In Runtime Manager, click the name of your application to view the dashboard.
2.  Click **Object Store** in the navigation menu.
3.  Click the object store, the partition, and then the key:

    ![Runtime Manager window showing the Object Store navigation menu, and the selected object store, partition, key, and key data](_images/os-rtm-key-in-os.png)

    <table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Object Store navigation menu</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Selected key</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>Key data (binary value and type)</td></tr></tbody></table>

    The `TestKey01` key that you sent from `curl` appears in the object store with the value
    `[binary value] BINARY`. The value is binary because Mule 4 wraps values in a Mule object, which
    causes them to be visible only as binary in Anypoint Platform.

4.  To delete a key, hover over a key name and click the trash can icon.

    Similarly, you can delete a partition or the store itself by hovering over and clicking the
    trash can icon.

    > [!IMPORTANT] Do not delete object stores that are associated with a running app. To prevent
    > errors, stop the app before deleting an object store.

The tutorial provides more detailed information about creating object stores in Mule 4 applications.

## Configure a Custom Time to Live Period

A custom time to live (TTL) period applies to the partition defined in the object store
configuration.

For information how rolling TTL behaves, see [Rolling TTL](./#rolling-ttl).

To configure a custom TTL period for keys stored in the object store:

1.  Create an app that uses Mule runtime engine version 4.2.1 or later.
2.  Configure the object store in the app.
3.  Select the **Store** operation in the flow and, in the properties window, click the green plus
    icon (+) for the **Object store** field:

    ![Studio properties window for the Store operation showing the green plus icon](_images/os-use-ttl-props.png)

4.  Configure the TTL entries in the **Global Object Store Configuration** window:

    ![Global Object Store Configuration window showing the Entry ttl field](_images/os-use-ttl-global-props.png)

    > [!NOTE] For Object Store v2, the **Max entries** field isnât configurable.
    - **Entry ttl**

      For Mule 4.2.1 and later, enter a custom TTL value of 0 through 2592000 seconds (30 days).

      If **Entry ttl** isnât empty, the TTL is static.

      If you leave this field empty, you enable rolling TTL; if you exceed 2592000 seconds, the TTL
      defaults to 2592000 seconds.

      For Mule versions earlier than 4.2.1, leave this field empty to set the TTL to 2592000
      seconds.

    - **Entry ttl unit**

      Specify the TTL time interval, such as MINUTES.

    - **Expiration interval**

      Specify how frequently to run the expiration thread.

      Set this parameter to a positive value that is less than the **Entry ttl** value. If this
      field is blank or the value is less than or equal to zero, Object Store v2 ignores **Entry
      ttl**.

    - **Expiration interval unit**

      Specify the expiration interval time unit, such as MINUTES.

5.  Click **OK** to dismiss the window.

## Create Object Store Partitions

You can use _partitions_ to associate multiple object stores with an app.

To create partitions in Object Store v2:

1.  Create an app and add a Store operation to the flow.
2.  Configure the Store operation.
3.  Configure the partitions:
    1.  In the properties window, click the green plus icon (+) for the **Object store** field:

        ![Studio properties window for the Store operation showing the green plus icon](_images/os-use-ttl-props.png)

    2.  In the **Global Element Properties** window, click the green plus icon (+) for
        **Configuration Reference** to create a global object store configuration:

        ![Global Element Properties window showing the green plus icon for the Configuration Reference field](_images/os-use-partition-studio7-os-global-props.png)

    3.  Name the object store configuration and click **OK**.
    4.  Ensure that the correct configuration reference is selected and click **OK**.
    5.  Ensure that the correct object store is selected and click **OK**.

4.  Repeat Step 3 for each partition that you want to create.
5.  Deploy the app to CloudHub and send a key-value pair to the object store.
6.  In Runtime Manager, click **Applications**, select the app, and click **Object Store** in the
    left navigation pane.
7.  Select the object store.

    The partitions associated with the object store appear in the **Object Store** page:

    ![Runtime Manager window showing Partitions in the Object Store page](_images/os-use-partitions-rtm.png)

## Deploy App with Object Store v2 Using the Mule Maven Plugin

If you are using a Mule Maven plugin version between 3.3.3 and 3.4.0 to deploy your app to CloudHub:

1.  Add the `<objectStoreV2>` element to your `pom.xml` project file:

    ```xml
    <cloudHubDeployment>
      ...
        <objectStoreV2>true</objectStoreV2>
      ...
    </cloudHubDeployment>
    ```

2.  Select **Use Object Store v2** in the **Deploy Application** page in Runtime Manager.

## See Also

- [Tutorial: Object Store v2](osv2-tutorial)
- [Rolling TTL](./#rolling-ttl)
- [What are the advantages of using partitions?](osv2-faq#what-are-the-advantages-of-using-partitions)
- [Object Store v2 API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/object-store-v2/)
- [Object Store v2 Stats API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/object-store-v2-stats/)
- [Object Store v2 Release Notes](../release-notes/object-store/anypoint-osv2-release-notes)
- [Object Store Connector for Mule 4](../object-store-connector/latest/)
- [Deploy Applications to CloudHub](../mule-runtime/latest/deploy-to-cloudhub)
---

# Object Store v2 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following release notes are provided for Object Store:

- [Object Store v2 Release Notes](../release-notes/object-store/anypoint-osv2-release-notes)
- [Object Store Connector Release Notes (for Mule 4)](../release-notes/connector/object-store-connector-release-notes-mule-4)
